extracted_documents.title: Chair Scrutiny Work Programme 202526 updated 07 Oct 2025

motions.title: Crisis and Resilience Grant
motions.full_text: To seek approval to deliver the Crisis and Resilience Fund from April 2026.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition may argue about the allocation of funds or the effectiveness of the programme.
motions.ruling_group_response: Highlight the successful delivery of the Household Support Fund and the need for continued support during the cost-of-living crisis.

amendment_predictions.amendment_angle: Increase funding for specific community initiatives.
amendment_predictions.counter: Absorb with a friendly amendment to include a review of funding distribution.

motions.title: Building Control Enforcement Policy 2026
motions.full_text: To seek approval from the Executive to adopt and implement the Building Control Enforcement Policy 2026.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition may argue about the enforcement mechanisms or the impact on local businesses.
motions.ruling_group_response: Emphasise the need for clear and consistent enforcement to ensure public safety and compliance with building regulations.

amendment_predictions.amendment_angle: Increase transparency in the enforcement process.
amendment_predictions.counter: Absorb with a friendly amendment to include a review of transparency measures.

motions.title: Empty Properties
motions.full_text: To consider a scheme within the Empty Property Project.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition may argue about the use of Compulsory Purchase Orders or the impact on property owners.
motions.ruling_group_response: Highlight the success of the Empty Homes Programme and the need to address long-term vacant properties.

amendment_predictions.amendment_angle: Increase community consultation before making Compulsory Purchase Orders.
amendment_predictions.counter: Absorb with a friendly amendment to include a review of community consultation processes.

questions.question_text: What specific measures will be taken to ensure the funds are distributed fairly and effectively?
questions.trap_explanation: Could be used to highlight perceived inequalities in funding distribution.

questions.question_text: How will the Council ensure that enforcement actions are transparent and fair to all parties involved?
recommended_answer_points: Clear standards of service and performance
questions.trap_explanation: Could be used to highlight the need for transparency in enforcement actions.

questions.question_text: What steps will be taken to ensure that community consultation is thorough and meaningful before making Compulsory Purchase Orders?
recommended_answer_points: Engagement with local residents and stakeholders
questions.trap_explanation: Could be used to highlight perceived lack of community consultation.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Opposition proposes a recorded vote on the Crisis and Resilience Grant motion.
tactical_scenarios.counter: Use a friendly amendment to include a review of funding distribution to gain opposition support.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Opposition argues for more detailed consultation on the Building Control Enforcement Policy 2026.
tactical_scenarios.counter: Absorb with a friendly amendment to include a review of consultation processes.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Opposition proposes a recorded vote on the Empty Properties motion.
tactical_scenarios.counter: Use a friendly amendment to include a review of community consultation processes to gain opposition support.

time_management.recommended_time_saving: Use friendly amendments on motions 1 and 3

extracted_documents.title: Appeals received and determined up to the 03.03.2026 - 12th Mar 2026 DC Cttee

motions.title: Approval of Minutes
motions.full_text: That the Minutes of the meeting held on 12th February 2026 be approved as a correct record and signed by the Chair.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition may attempt to challenge the accuracy of the minutes, particularly regarding the vote on FUL/2025/0464 if they believe it was misrepresented.
motions.ruling_group_response: Affirm the accuracy of the minutes. Remind members that the Chair's signature confirms the record. Unless a factual inaccuracy can be demonstrated, the minutes should be approved as presented.

motions.title: VAR/2024/0241 - Variation of Conditions for Battery Storage Proposal
motions.full_text: That application VAR/2024/0241 for the variation of conditions 2, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16, 18 and removal of Condition 20 attached to full planning permission reference FUL/2021/0375 to allow change in levels at Land At Balderstone Lane, Burnley BB10 2TS, be approved subject to conditions.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition will likely argue that the proposed variations, particularly the change in levels to create terraced platforms, do not adequately address or may worsen the impacts of the original development. They will highlight the 'more than three objections' received and the fact that the original proposal was a 'departure from the policies within the Local Plan'. They may also question the retrospective element of the application, framing it as the developer attempting to regularise work already commenced without full prior consent. Key opposition members from the previous meeting (G Birtwistle, S Hussain, J Inckle, L Khan, J Launer) are highly likely to speak against approval, potentially moving for refusal.
motions.ruling_group_response: Emphasise that the original planning permission FUL/2021/0375 for the battery storage is extant and cannot be revisited. The committee's decision is solely on the proposed variations to conditions. Stress that officers have assessed the changes as acceptable on planning grounds, and that the terraced approach is intended to reduce material removal, potentially lessening environmental impact. Reiterate the Legal Advisor's warning from the previous meeting: any decision contrary to officer recommendation must be based on robust, defensible planning grounds to avoid potential costs being awarded against the Council. Highlight the positive and proactive approach of the Local Planning Authority as per NPPF paragraph 38.

amendment_predictions.likely_proposer: Councillor G Birtwistle (Liberal Democrats) or Councillor S Hussain (Independent)
amendment_predictions.amendment_angle: A motion to refuse the application, contrary to officer recommendation, citing reasons related to visual impact, environmental concerns, or perceived non-compliance with local plan policies, or the retrospective nature of the application.
amendment_predictions.counter: Demand clear, specific planning reasons for refusal that relate directly to the *variations* proposed, not the principle of the original development. Have the Legal Advisor re-read the costs warning. Argue that refusing this variation could force the developer to revert to the original, potentially less desirable, approved method of construction. If reasons are vague, suggest deferral for officers to prepare a detailed response to proposed refusal grounds.

key_data_points: Original permission FUL/2021/0375 for 152MWh battery storage is extant.
S.73 application to vary conditions 2, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16, 18 and remove condition 20.
Changes relate to ground levels (terraced platforms vs. single flat site) to reduce material removal.
Original application was a 'departure from the policies within the Local Plan'.
Work has commenced on site, making this partly retrospective.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: A motion to refuse application VAR/2024/0241 is moved, contrary to officer recommendation.
tactical_scenarios.counter: Immediately request the proposer to articulate clear, specific, and robust planning reasons for refusal, referencing relevant development plan policies or material considerations that are breached by the *proposed variations*. Have the Legal Advisor reiterate the costs warning, emphasising that the reasons must be defensible at appeal. Highlight that the original permission is extant, and refusing this variation might not stop the development but could lead to a less desirable outcome under the original consent. Prepare for a named vote.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Standard committee procedure for motions and amendments. Named vote likely if motion is contrary to officer advice.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Members of the public or a non-committee ward councillor (e.g., from Briercliffe Ward) speak against VAR/2024/0241 under the Right to Speak Policy.
tactical_scenarios.counter: Listen respectfully. The Chair should remind committee members that while public views are important, decisions must be based on material planning considerations. Officers should be prepared to address specific points raised by speakers, clarifying how the application (or its conditions) addresses those concerns, or why certain points are not material planning considerations.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Right to Speak Policy (as per previous minutes), time limits for speakers.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: A committee member proposes deferral of VAR/2024/0241, seeking further information or clarification.
tactical_scenarios.counter: Argue against deferral unless genuinely new, material planning issues are raised that require further officer investigation. Emphasise that officers have thoroughly assessed the application. If deferral is unavoidable, ensure the reasons are specific, time-bound, and clearly define what further information is required.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Standard committee procedure for motions to defer.

time_management.recommended_time_saving: Focus debate on VAR/2024/0241 strictly on the proposed variations and material planning considerations, avoiding re-litigation of the original permission.

speakers.name: Councillor M Ishtiaq
key_points: Propose approval of minutes.

key_points: Present officer report and recommendation for approval.
Clarify scope of s.73 application and limitations.
Address public objections with planning rationale.
Explain benefits of terraced approach.

speakers.name: Ward Councillor (Briercliffe)
key_points: Voice local concerns and objections to the proposal.

speakers.name: Public Speakers
key_points: Present objections from local residents.

speakers.name: Committee Member (Proposer for Approval)
key_points: Propose approval as per officer recommendation.
Emphasise planning merits of the variation.
Highlight officer advice and legal implications of refusal.

speakers.name: Councillor G Birtwistle
key_points: Argue against approval, focusing on public objections, retrospective element, and potential negative impacts of the changes.
Question compliance with local plan policies.

speakers.name: Councillor S Hussain
key_points: Echo public concerns, particularly regarding local amenity or environmental impact.
Challenge the planning grounds for approval of the variations.

speakers.name: Councillor J Inckle
key_points: Focus on procedural aspects or perceived lack of transparency regarding the retrospective element.
Support refusal based on planning grounds.

speakers.name: Councillor L Khan
key_points: Raise concerns about the overall impact of the development on the area, even with variations.
Call for stronger conditions or refusal.

speakers.name: Councillor J Launer
key_points: Focus on environmental or ecological impacts of the changes, or the original 'departure from Local Plan' status.
Advocate for refusal or deferral.

speakers.name: Sattar Hussain
key_points: Provide legal advice, especially regarding costs warnings for decisions contrary to officer recommendation without robust planning grounds.

media_risk_items: VAR/2024/0241 - Battery storage proposal due to public objections, retrospective element, and potential for committee to vote against officer advice and incur appeal costs.
